failures_details.output:
--- shfmt drift ---
diff ./plugins/rq/scripts/rq-next.sh.orig ./plugins/rq/scripts/rq-next.sh
--- ./plugins/rq/scripts/rq-next.sh.orig
+++ ./plugins/rq/scripts/rq-next.sh
@@ -238,9 +238,9 @@
     # RQ_EXEC_BYPASS_TTY=1 is a TEST-ONLY escape hatch (see rq-start.sh for
     # the full rationale). Production callers MUST NOT set it.
     if [ ! -t 0 ] && [ "${RQ_EXEC_BYPASS_TTY:-0}" != 1 ]; then
-      :   # not a TTY and no test bypass — silently skip
+      : # not a TTY and no test bypass — silently skip
     elif ! command -v claude >/dev/null 2>&1; then
-      :   # claude not on PATH — silently skip
+      : # claude not on PATH — silently skip
     else
       exec claude
     fi
diff ./plugins/rq/scripts/rq-start.sh.orig ./plugins/rq/scripts/rq-start.sh
--- ./plugins/rq/scripts/rq-start.sh.orig
+++ ./plugins/rq/scripts/rq-start.sh
@@ -223,9 +223,9 @@
     # under non-interactive input (CI, piped invocations) and replace the
     # shell with a hung/failing claude process.
     if [ ! -t 0 ] && [ "${RQ_EXEC_BYPASS_TTY:-0}" != 1 ]; then
-      :   # not a TTY and no test bypass — silently skip
+      : # not a TTY and no test bypass — silently skip
     elif ! command -v claude >/dev/null 2>&1; then
-      :   # claude not on PATH — silently skip
+      : # claude not on PATH — silently skip
     else
       exec claude
     fi
